NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Muhammad Shah
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The twenty-ninth Mughal Emperor and the last to rule a nominally united Hindustan, Muhammad Shah inherited a crumbling empire and a court in open chaos. Known as Rangila, the Colourful, for his love of music, art, and poetry, he is not the warrior the moment demands. Yet behind the hedonism lies a canny survivor who has outlasted multiple coups and palace intrigues. He knows he cannot fight the Marathas, the Afghans, and his own Nawabs simultaneously, so he haggles, delays, and plays factions against each other, buying time for an empire whose time may already have passed.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Murshid Quli Khan
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The first independent Nawab of Bengal and its most capable administrator, Murshid Quli Khan built Bengal into the wealthiest province in the known world. A converted Hindu orphan raised at the Mughal court, his genius was for revenue collection and commercial policy. He restructured the zamindari system, imposed disciplined tax reforms, and drew the European trading companies into arrangements that made Bengal prosper without making it dependent on them. He rules the province as a private kingdom while paying nominal deference to Delhi. The fiction of Mughal suzerainty satisfies both parties.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Asaf Jah I
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Mir Qamar ud-Din Khan, titled Asaf Jah and Nizam ul-Mulk, is perhaps the most capable administrator alive in Hindostan. A veteran of four emperors courts, he escaped the viper pit of Delhi to carve out the Deccan Plateau as his own domain. Methodical, cold-blooded, and patient, Asaf Jah combined Persian administrative traditions with locally recruited Deccani cavalry to build Hyderabad into a formidable state. He views the Maratha Confederacy as the primary existential threat and has organised his entire kingdom around containing it. He legitimizes his de facto independence by always bowing, very formally, toward Shahjahanabad.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Ahmad Shah Durrani
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Born of the Abdali clan of Pashtuns, Ahmad Shah rose through the ranks of the Persian imperial army to command its finest Afghan cavalry contingent. A natural horseman with a generals instinct, he united the fractious Pashtun tribes under his banner through equal measures of iron diplomacy and relentless force. His vision extends far beyond raiding the plains of India. He intends to build a lasting Durrani Sultanate that controls the great mountain passes between east and west, commanding the trade routes that have made conquerors rich since Alexander marched through Kandahar.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Krishnaraja Wodeyar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The ruling Maharaja of Mysore and heir to an ancient dynasty tracing its lineage to the medieval Yadava kings, Krishnaraja Wodeyar represents the sacred legitimacy of the Gaddi of Srirangapatna. A man of gentle disposition and genuine scholarly learning, his reign has been increasingly shaped by the military ambitions of his powerful Dalvai commanders. Under Krishnaraja, Mysore has expanded its revenue base, reformed its armies, and asserted independence from both the Mughal Deccan Sultanates and the Maratha Confederacy. He rules from the throne; his commanders have made that throne something others fear.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Tipu Sultan
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Son of Hyder Ali and already acclaimed as a commander of rare brilliance, Tipu Sultan has mastered both traditional Nayaka cavalry tactics and modern musketry with a speed that astonishes veterans twice his age. His particular genius lies in rocket warfare: he has championed iron-cased rockets deployed in massed batteries as a weapon to break enemy formations before cavalry closes. Deeply religious and fiercely independent, Tipu understands what few other lords yet grasp: that the European trading companies arriving on Indias coasts are not merely merchants, but the vanguard of something far more dangerous. He has made it his lifes work to stop them.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Hyder Ali
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A soldier who rose from an ordinary trooper to Dalvai of Mysore by sheer talent, Hyder Ali cannot read or write, yet his grasp of logistics, terrain, and human nature is extraordinary. He rebuilt Mysores infantry on European lines, hired French officers to train his gunners, and developed supply chains that allow his armies to campaign in terrain that would starve any other force. His strategic vision is decades ahead of his contemporaries: he understands that the coming wars will be won by economic organisation and disciplined musketry, not ancestral cavalry charges. Utterly self-made, he is the true architect of Mysores power.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Sawai Jai Singh II
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The Maharaja of Jaipur and paramount leader of the Rajputana Federation, Sawai Jai Singh is a man who contains multitudes. As a warrior, he commanded Mughal cavalry with distinction across the Deccan campaigns. As a scholar, he constructed five great astronomical observatories, the Jantar Mantars, and corresponded with European scientists as an intellectual equal. As a politician, he has navigated the collapse of Mughal authority with greater skill than any of his peers, using marriages, alliances, and patient negotiation where others reached for the sword. He knows the age of pure martial tradition is ending, and he is positioning Rajputana for the world that comes next.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Bajirao I
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The Peshwa of the Maratha Confederacy and its greatest battlefield commander, Bajirao I has never lost a battle. From his early twenties he has commanded the Maratha horse with a genius that is simultaneously terrifying and elegant, moving armies the way a chess master moves pieces, always three turns ahead. His strategy of Hindu Pad Padshahi, a Hindu sovereign empire of Hindostan, is both military objective and political creed. Of magnetic personal courage and extraordinary physical endurance, Bajirao represents a new kind of Indian leadership: not the inherited kingship of the Rajputs, but authority earned through unbroken victory and the loyalty of men who would follow him into the jaws of death.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}Charat Singh Sukerchakia
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Sirdar of the Sukerchakia Misl and one of the most formidable commanders of the Sikh Dal Khalsa, Charat Singh built his warband from a handful of horsemen into a force that dominates the Rechna Doab between the Chenab and Ravi rivers. He combines the Khalsa codes egalitarianism with a chieftains ruthless pragmatism, surviving assassination attempts, imperial campaigns, and inter-Misl feuds to emerge as the dominant figure in the confederation. Students of history note that it is his bloodline, through his grandson Ranjit Singh, that will one day unite all the Misls into a single Sikh empire. Charat Singh himself is already, unknowingly, laying that foundation.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Danishmand
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Educated at the Mughal court as a munshi, {FIRSTNAME} left imperial service after witnessing one too many purges of loyal secretaries. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} command of written Persian, coded correspondence, and court protocol makes {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} invaluable to any lord seeking to navigate the politics of Hindostan.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Pehlawar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A champion of the kushti wrestling pit who has competed before three Nawabs, {FIRSTNAME} travels the circuits of Hindostan taking on all challengers. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} prodigious strength and iron constitution translate directly from the pit to the battlefield.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Khamosh
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} says almost nothing, observes everything, and has survived three separate assassination attempts that killed everyone around {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?}. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} does not explain how.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Sher Dil
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A former Sowar cavalry officer who refused a direct order to massacre a village, {FIRSTNAME} left imperial service with nothing but a horse and a clear conscience. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} has since made a living fighting for those who cannot fight for themselves.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Gharat Gar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} has burned three towns and will explain at length why each one deserved it, if given the chance. There are those who disagree, which is why {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} is no longer welcome in those provinces.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Qassab
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for a trade {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} once practised, {FIRSTNAME} found that the required skills, calm efficiency and a steady hand under pressure, translate remarkably well to war. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} seldom speaks of the transition.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Pahari
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Born in the Himalayan foothills, {FIRSTNAME} came down to the plains seeking fortune and found mostly conflict. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} knowledge of mountain terrain and high passes has saved many employers from disasters they never saw coming.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Qalandar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A wandering Sufi dervish who answers to no worldly authority and follows no predictable path, {FIRSTNAME} fights when the spirit moves {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?}. The spirit, it turns out, is moved quite frequently.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Mardud
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Expelled from {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} clan for reasons {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} calls political and others call criminal, {FIRSTNAME} has made peace with the outsider life. The work is steady and the questions are few.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Siyah
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Known across the Deccan bazaars for {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} black armor and midnight raids, {FIRSTNAME} was a shadow operative until {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} patron decided {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} had grown too capable to retain and too dangerous to dismiss. The situation resolved itself in {FIRSTNAME}s favour.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Hakim
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A physician trained in the Yunani tradition, {FIRSTNAME} drifts between campaigns treating wounds and asking uncomfortable questions about how they were inflicted. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} carries a sword for cases where the conversation goes poorly.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Beghar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} once owned a respectable house in Agra, three good horses, and a library. Now {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} owns the horses and has traded the rest for freedom, which {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} considers the better bargain.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Darya Dil
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A Pashtun chieftain who lost {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} clan in an inter-tribal feud, {FIRSTNAME} now leads a freelance warband across the frontier. The reckless courage that cost {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} the clan has paradoxically made {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} legendary among soldiers of fortune.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Sarkob
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Undefeated champion of three consecutive Eid wrestling festivals in Kandahar, {FIRSTNAME} found the transition from the pit to the battlefield straightforward. The principles are identical: close the distance, seize control, end it quickly.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Bakhtawar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}By every rational accounting, {FIRSTNAME} should have died at least nine times. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} maintains a tally and attributes the outcome to divine favour. Those who have fought beside {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} have stopped arguing with the conclusion.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Khun Rizan
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Enforcer for three successive Khans, none of whom survived their tenure, {FIRSTNAME} is careful about new employers but has never been out of work. Word travels fast in the Afghan passes.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Daryai
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A river-people fighter from the great waterways of the northwest frontier, {FIRSTNAME} is as capable on fast-moving water as on land. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} knowledge of crossings, fords, and seasonal floods has tipped more than one battle.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Ahangar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A master armorer who took up weapons after the destruction of {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} forge, {FIRSTNAME} knows the precise weak points in every type of armor by professional experience. It shows in combat.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Barfani
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Raised in the high passes above Kabul where winter kills the unprepared, {FIRSTNAME} fights best in the conditions that reduce other soldiers to uselessness. The cold is an old companion.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Malun
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Three warlords have sworn blood vengeance against {FIRSTNAME}. None have collected yet. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} has stopped counting and started wearing the name as a badge.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Qilahdar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A former fort commander who surrendered a mountain pass to the highest bidder and then discovered the bidder could not pay, {FIRSTNAME} now resolves to accept payment in advance and fight for coin rather than principle.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Khanjar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Short, fast, and lethal at arm reach, {FIRSTNAME} fights with paired long knives and a preference for spaces too confined for swords. Employers have learned to stand well back during the opening moments.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Panditji
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A Brahmin scholar who studied in the maths of Srirangapatna before the death of {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} patron left {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} without support, {FIRSTNAME} turned to arms reluctantly and proved disturbingly capable at them.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Suvarna
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Known for the gold jewelry {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} wears into battle, stripped from enemies {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} insists rather than purchased, {FIRSTNAME} treats war as a commercial enterprise and keeps meticulous accounts of the returns.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Rajali
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A former Nayaka cavalry officer dismissed for winning a battle through methods the generals called irregular, {FIRSTNAME} has since found that minor lords and merchants value results over military orthodoxy.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Khangila
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} is honest about not being sure what {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} is. The sword came naturally; everything else has been improvised. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} is at peace with the uncertainty.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Kumara
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Young, eager, and uncertain whether the stories of {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own bravery are accurate or simply what people decided to believe, {FIRSTNAME} keeps fighting partly to find out.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Tarakasi
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for the silver filigree craft of Cuttack that {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} mother practised, {FIRSTNAME} has the patience and precision of a master artisan applied to entirely different materials.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Aranyaka
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A forest-dwelling ascetic who left the jungle when logging companies arrived with Mughal permits and armed escorts, {FIRSTNAME} discovered that the skills of the hermit, stillness, acute observation, subsistence survival, are the skills of the scout.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Parivrajaka
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A perpetually wandering ascetic who has resolved no philosophical questions in twenty years of seeking, {FIRSTNAME} has stopped worrying about it and fights for whoever makes the most interesting proposition.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Vaishya
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A merchant robbed of everything by a corrupt toll-collector under Hyderabadi license, {FIRSTNAME} resolved the matter through channels that have since made official commerce unavailable. The recovered sum exceeded the original loss.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Veerabhadra
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for the fierce form of Shiva the Destroyer, {FIRSTNAME} takes the name literally. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} reputation in the Deccan is for absolute commitment to the objective regardless of what stands in the way.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Shilpi
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A master craftsperson who built siege engines for three kingdoms before concluding that the best way to learn how things break is to break them personally, {FIRSTNAME} is now available for demolition work of all varieties.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Kadva
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name means bitter, and {FIRSTNAME} has earned it through long experience with unreliable employers and broken promises. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} now demands half in advance and has not been surprised since.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Badnam
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Notoriously expelled from the Sisodia household troops for a reason the official record calls a misunderstanding, {FIRSTNAME} has built a second reputation in exile that has eclipsed the first.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Veera
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A straightforward warrior of remarkable physical capability who does not overthink objectives, {FIRSTNAME} has found this quality rarer and more valued than it sounds in the great courts of Rajputana.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Puratan
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A keeper of ancient fighting traditions predating the Mughal era, {FIRSTNAME} practises techniques from manuscripts most warriors consider folklore. The opponents who underestimated the old ways did not survive the lesson.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Makara
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for the sea-creature on the banner of a Rajput house that no longer exists, {FIRSTNAME} carried the name out of the ruins and has made it mean something new.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Manorath
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} pursued one specific goal for fifteen years and achieved it. The achievement proved unsatisfying. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} is now available and without fixed purpose, which makes {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} an excellent mercenary.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Pahadiya
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A hill-fort defender who held {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} position for seven months before being ordered to surrender by the very army {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} was defending, {FIRSTNAME} left that service and has not worked for any great noble since.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Shikarni
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Hunter by training, fighter by necessity, {FIRSTNAME} approaches battle with the patience of someone who has waited three days motionless for game that may never come, and remained still the entire time.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Lohar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A weaponsmith who grew tired of making swords for people with inferior skills, {FIRSTNAME} decided to demonstrate proper usage. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} maintains {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own equipment to standards that shame most armorers.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Kaalu
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name was a childhood taunt that {FIRSTNAME} reclaimed as {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} is direct about {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} origins, direct about {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} terms, and direct in combat. Complexity, {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} has concluded, is usually a form of dishonesty.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Gharela
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A former domestic servant who discovered, upon being conscripted into a Rajput household army, that {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} was considerably better at war than at household management. The army shared this assessment.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Agni Mukha
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name was given by survivors. {FIRSTNAME} set fire to a Maratha supply column three times the size of {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own force and made it away clean. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} considers it an ordinary days work.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Shikar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Hunter-born and accustomed to patience and sudden violence in alternation, {FIRSTNAME} finds the rhythms of mercenary work comfortable. The pay is better than game and the prey at least fights back.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Vaidya
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}An Ayurvedic physician who served Maratha field armies for a decade before deciding that preventing wounds was more interesting than treating them, {FIRSTNAME} now leads rather than follows.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Lal
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Red-turbaned and impossible to miss on a battlefield, {FIRSTNAME} treats the visibility as a tactical asset: if enemies are focusing on {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?}, they are focusing on the wrong target.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Suvarachuda
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} gold-crested helmet, which {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} will fight any challenger to keep, {FIRSTNAME} has won that particular argument thirty-seven times and is not interested in losing the thirty-eighth.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Tambdi
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The deepest red, the colour of Maratha battle-flags, is how {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} clan describes the hue of {FIRSTNAME}s reputation across the Deccan. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} takes it as a compliment.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Ghoda Chor
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A former cavalry raider whose epithet arose from a series of incidents considerably more complicated than the name suggests, {FIRSTNAME} is now employed strictly as a legitimate fighting force. The emphasis on strictly is {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Pheratwala
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}An itinerant trader who abandoned commerce after realising that the profits from walking between settlements went almost entirely to toll-collectors and bandits, {FIRSTNAME} has resolved to be the one doing the collecting.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Kaladant
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name was given young and has stuck. {FIRSTNAME} has made no effort to correct the reputation it implies, finding it useful to have opponents unsettled before the first blow.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Bhramanar
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A wanderer by philosophy and practice, {FIRSTNAME} has crossed the Deccan six times in search of something {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} cannot name. The search has made {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} extraordinarily familiar with every terrain, road, and pass between the Vindhyas and the Cauvery.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Manhoos
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Three times {FIRSTNAME} has joined a military campaign that subsequently failed catastrophically. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} is untroubled by the pattern and notes that {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} personally survived all three. Potential employers draw their own conclusions.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Gauri Chor
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name attached after an incident involving a temple treasury, a corrupt administrator, and circumstances that {FIRSTNAME} explains as a misunderstanding of jurisdiction. The recovered funds went to the village; this is not disputed.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Gyani
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}A scholar of the Granth Sahib who left the gurdwara after concluding that the enemies of the Khalsa would not be defeated by reading alone, {FIRSTNAME} fights with a ferocity that surprises those who expect a philosopher.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Baaz
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Named for the falcon on the Khalsa battle-standard, {FIRSTNAME} fights with the birds qualities: speed, precision, and a willingness to strike something three times {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own size.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Anath
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}An orphan of Mughal reprisals against Sikh villages, {FIRSTNAME} was raised by the Dal Khalsa warband that found {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} in the ruins. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} has been fighting ever since and has no plans to stop.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Lohit Nayan
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The red-eyed warrior of the Sikh Misls has a reputation that travels ahead of {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} into every engagement. {FIRSTNAME} encourages this: reputation is armor that costs nothing to maintain.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Nikala
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} escaped from an imperial jail in Lahore under circumstances {?HERO.GENDER}she{?}he{\?} has never fully explained. The jail was considerably less intact after the escape than before. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} declines further details.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Pagal
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name was intended as an insult. {FIRSTNAME} accepted it as an accurate description of the willingness to fight any odds without calculation. {?HERO.GENDER}Her{?}His{\?} survival record suggests the calculation was being done by someone.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Dhusari Baaz
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The grey falcon of the Punjab, {FIRSTNAME} is older than most warriors in {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} trade and considerably more dangerous. Experience has not slowed {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?}; it has made {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}him{\?} more efficient.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Baghin
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The tigress earned {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} name during a patrol engagement in the Himalayan foothills that the official Mughal report describes as an ambush. The Mughal commanders who survived it use a different word.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Akela
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The lone wolf who fought alone for three years after {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} warband was destroyed, {FIRSTNAME} has come to accept that working alongside others is occasionally necessary. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} remains precise about the terms.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Tez
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}Fast, sharp, and impatient with everything that is neither, {FIRSTNAME} ends fights in moments that other warriors would stretch into hours. Employers appreciate the economy; opponents rarely have time to form an opinion.

NPCCharacter.name: {=!}{FIRSTNAME} Gudriya
NPCCharacter.text: {=!}The name is Punjabi for a rag doll, a joke about {FIRSTNAME}s appearance that the jokers no longer repeat. {?HERO.GENDER}She{?}He{\?} took the name as {?HERO.GENDER}her{?}his{\?} own and made it something they remember with more complicated feelings.
